response: 15397 is not prime because it equals 89 × 173.
reasoning_content: We need to determine if 15397 is prime. Quick sanity check: check divisibility by small primes. 15397 is odd, not divisible by 2. Sum of digits: 1+5+3+9+7=25, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3. Last digit not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5. Check 7: 7*2199=15393, remainder 4, so not divisible. Check 11: alternating sum: 1-5+3-9+7 = -3, not divisible by 11. Check 13: 13*1184=15392, remainder 5. Check 17: 17*905=15385, remainder 12. Check 19: 19*810=15390, remainder 7. Check 23: 23*669=15387, remainder 10. Check 29: 29*531=15399, remainder -2, so 29*530=15370, remainder 27. Not. Check 31: 31*496=15376, remainder 21. Check 37: 37*416=15392, remainder 5. Check 41: 41*375=15375, remainder 22. Check 43: 43*358=15394, remainder 3. Check 47: 47*327=15369, remainder 28. Check 53: 53*290=15370, remainder 27. Check 59: 59*261=15399, remainder -2, so 59*260=15340, remainder 57. Not. Check 61: 61*252=15372, remainder 25. Check 67: 67*229=15343, remainder 54. Check 71: 71*216=15336, remainder 61. Check 73: 73*211=15403, too high, 73*210=15330, remainder 67. Check 79: 79*195=15405, too high, 79*194=15326, remainder 71. Check 83: 83*185=15355, remainder 42. Check 89: 89*173=15397? 89*173 = 89*170 + 89*3 = 15130 + 267 = 15397 exactly! So 15397 = 89 * 173. Therefore not prime. Quick sanity: 89 is prime, 173 is prime? Check if 173 is prime: not divisible by 2,3,5,7 (7*24=168), 11 (11*15=165), 13 (13*13=169), 17 (17*10=170), so 173 is prime. So composite. Answer: not prime.
